records.ety: [F. oppresser, LL. oppressare, fr. L. oppressus, p. p. of opprimere; ob (see Ob-) + premere to press. See Press.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Oppressed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Oppressing.]

defs: To impose excessive burdens upon; to overload; hence, to treat with unjust rigor or with cruelty.
quotes: For thee, oppressd king, am I cast down.
Behold the kings of the earth; how they oppress Thy chosen!

defs: To put down; to crush out; to suppress.
quotes: The mutiny he there hastes to oppress.

defs: To produce a sensation of weight in (some part of the body); as, my lungs are oppressed by the damp air; excess of food oppresses the stomach.
